column1: # fqxv benchmark datasets — short-read Illumina, spanning both quality regimes.
# Fetched with `sracha` (see fetch.sh). Columns:
#   accession  label  platform  layout  quality  approx_gz  reference
# `reference` is a genome FASTA URL used only by concordance.sh (the downstream
# variant-call fidelity check); `-` = no reference (skipped). Only DNA
# resequencing datasets get one — RNA-seq needs splice-aware calling (out of
# scope), so those stay `-`.
# ERR10213669 dropped: reference-compressed cSRA, not reconstructable by sracha.
# Add a plain (unaligned) human NovaSeq run here if a binned-human sample is wanted.
# Long-read (Oxford Nanopore) — single-end, wide quality range (Phred ~0-90),
# ~14 kb mean read length. sracha writes this as DRR205413.fastq (no _1 suffix);
# run_bench.sh falls back to that name for single-end layouts.
# Long-read (PacBio HiFi) — E. coli E2348/69, same DNA as the Wick 2021
# comparison (BioProject PRJNA602597, run SRR11434954, HiFi/CCS). The full run
# is ~23 Gbp (~5000x); longread_hifi.sbatch subsamples it to ~120k reads
# (~1.5 Gbp, still ~300x) into $DATA_DIR/ecoli_hifi.fastq. Narrow high-Q, low
# error — the regime where the cross-read DNA lever matters most.
ecoli_hifi
